rulings.Case Title: CARNELL GAINES et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER, LLC'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Hearing required. Plaintiff to address when the deposition word searches described in paragraphs 6 and 9 (Acuna Declaration) occurred and explain what fact made it apparent that Mr. Jimenez knew Mr. Gaines per paragraph 10 (Id.). Defendant to address why continuing the motion to allow defendant to depose these witnesses fails to cure any prejudice incurred by defendant in preparing for trial. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: DEBRA LEMMONS et al VS. JACKIE ROBINSON GARDEN APARTMENTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANTS ROBERT FERRIS AND ROBERT BOCKELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. Plaintiff's inability to present expert testimony is a new fact and defendant has shifted the burden by demonstrating that expert testimony is necessary to prove plaintiff's claim. Expert testimony is necessary to prove the property is subject to the Rent Control Ordinance and to explain why plaintiff did not waive the eviction claim when signing the stipulation and judgment. These issues must be addressed before plaintiff can assert that defendant gave erroneous advice regarding the statute of limitations. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections are overruled. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY WALTHER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT D.W. NICHOLSON CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of proof based on factually devoid discovery. Given the Court's ruling on the Robbins' declaration, plaintiff fails to present admissible evidence that creates triable issue of fact. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: LARRY WALTHER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Strike The Decl Of Gordon Robbins And To Exclude Testimony Of Gordon Robbins
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 11. DEFENDANT D.W. NICHOLSON CORPORATION'S MOTION To Strike The Declaration Of Gordon Robbins And To Exclude Testimony Of Gordon Robbins is granted. The declaration contradicts Robbins' deposition testimony. D'Amico v. Board of Medical Examiners (1974) 11 Cal. 3d. 1, 21. Plaintiff has failed to explain why Robbins did not appear at his noticed deposition. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES G GIRARD VS. TODD ROBBINS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defts' Motion To Strike (Ccp 425.16); Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Defts' Special Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANTS AMEY NUTTER, TODD ROBINS, DELPHINE RENARD, CHRISTOPHER NUTTER AND TIMOTHY ZAMBELLI'S Motion To Strike (CCP 425.16) is denied. Only the first two causes of action implicate the anti-SLAPP statute. Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence to establish a prima facie case. The dismissal is a favorable termination; the lawyer's advise is not determinative given the facts related to plaintiff's notice of a disability and the content of the Blumefeld memo; and the facts set forth in paragraph 23 of Girard's declaration provide a basis for finding malice. Defendant fails to demonstrate that the remaining causes of action are covered by CCP 425.16. The gravaman of those claims embrace non-litigation conduct. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN HANNON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, OFFICER JOHN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend as to the 1st cause of action which is barred by the statute of limitations. Maldonado v. Harris (2004) 370 F. 3d 945, 954. The demurrer to the 9th cause of action is sustained. Plaintiff has failed to allege facts that support the claim that he was denied benefits as a consequence of his disability. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: SEAN HANNON VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, OFFICER JOHN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is granted because the Court previously sustained defendant's demurrer to those causes of action without leave to amend. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF THE WEST VS. MONSTER MAGIC, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Change Of Dept And Time For Hearing And Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default And To Set Aside Deault Judgemnt
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANTS MONSTER MAGIC, LLC AND STEVEN MCVAY'S Notice Of Change Of Dept And Time For Hearing And Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default And To Set Aside Default Judgment is denied. The parties were served and failed to take appropriate steps to respond to the complaint. Defendants' declarations fail to establish a basis for excusable neglect. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: EDDY GUTIERREZ et al VS. PAUL VOSE et al

rulings.Case Title: JASON GRANT GARZA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. Res judicata, based on plaintiff's prior claim, bars this action. The claims also are barred by the statute of limitations, the failure to file a timely claim and the litigation privilege as it relates to Nurse Lewis' testimony. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration0
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 12, 2008, line 9. PETITIONER TRANSPORT WORKERS UNION LOCAL 200'S Petition To Compel Arbitration. The motion to compel arbitration is denied. The MOU, relied on by Petitioner, was in effect during 2006-2007 fiscal year. Petitioner has stated unequivocally that "there was no violation of the agreement for fiscal year 2006-2007". It also is undisputed that the parties entered into a subsquent MOU governing the rights and obligations of the parties after the expiration to the 2006-2007 agreeement. As the June 19, 2007 grievance makes clear, Petitioner seeks to arbitrate a breach that occurs after the 2006-2007 contract has ended but is not seeking to arbitrate the issue under the subsequent agreement; rather Petitioner seeks to arbitrate the dispute based on an agreement that Petitioner admits has not been violated. It follows, therefore, that since the agreement relied upon by Petitioner admittedly has not been breached, there is no basis to compel arbitration. It would simply be an idle act. There also is a material issue as to Petitioner's waiver of this claim given the April 12, 2007 grievance.=(302/PJM/VC)
